business.headline: Buying a car or bike? The hidden truth behind insurance sold at dealershipsWhen you buy a car or bike, the final bill usually comes as an 'on-road price' that includes insurance. For most buyers, this is accepted without much thought, as it appears to be a standard part of the purchase. But what often goes unnoticed is that this insurance is not mandatory to buy from the dealer.

Middle East war to cut global growth, spike inflation despite truce: World BankThe World Bank's baseline estimate now projects growth in emerging markets and developing economies of 3.65 per cent in 2026, compared to 4 per cent in October, dropping as low as 2.6 per cent in an adverse scenario with a longer-lasting war.

India allows 4 sanctioned tankers carrying Iranian oil to berth at Sikka: ReportIranian oil is often transported by a so-called shadow fleet of vessels that lack internationally recognised insurance and safety certifications. But this requires special permission from the government as exemptions are required under Indian rules for the berthing of ships.

Gold dips, silver slips: Is this the right time to invest or wait?Prices of gold and silver have cooled off after recent highs, but the bigger question remains, is this the right entry point, or is there more downside ahead?

Why April is the best time to reset your budget, SIPs and other investmentsIn Part 2 of our MONEY MAKEOVER series, we explore how April, with salary hikes, bonuses and tax changes, creates the perfect window to pause, reset and move forward with clarity. Let's have a look.

RBI wants a 1-hour pause on some UPI payments. Here's whyThe Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released a discussion paper proposing a one-hour delay for certain digital payments. Here's an explainer on how it could work, which transactions it may affect, and what it means for users.

Sending over Rs 10,000 via UPI? RBI may soon introduce a 1-hour delayIf you often use UPI for bigger payments, a change may be on the way. The RBI is looking at adding a one-hour pause for transfers above Rs 10,000 to help prevent online fraud.

Applying for TAN? Income Tax Dept replaces Form 49B with two new formsApplying for a TAN is set to become a lot simpler. The Income Tax Department has replaced the old Form 49B with two new forms, aiming to make the process clearer and easier for different types of applicants. Let's have a look.

Wipro shares rise as board considers buyback after three yearsWipro has informed exchanges that its board will evaluate a share buyback alongside its fourth-quarter results on April 16, bringing the focus back on shareholder returns.

Ola Electric shares continue to rise, up 6% today. Here's whyAt 9:44 am, Ola Electric shares were trading at Rs 38.71, up Rs 2.37 or 6.52%. Yesterday, the stock closed nearly 20% higher.

general news.headline: Why Asim Munir met Iran leaders in combat gear but JD Vance in a suitPakistan army chief Asim Munir sent a carefully choreographed message through his choice of clothes while receiving the Iranian delegation and US Vice President JD Vance as both sides arrived in Islamabad on Saturday for make-or-break peace talks.

Live: JD Vance holds talks with Pak PM Shehbaz Sharif in IslamabadUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Iran's delegation, led by Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, arrived in Islamabad early Saturday for high-stakes talks with the US aimed at ending weeks of conflict. The visit comes amid growing urgency to stabilise the region, with President Donald Trump warning of intensified strikes if no agreement is reached. Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has called the talks a "make-or-break moment" after announcing a two-week ceasefire earlier this week. According to Tasnim news agency, the Iranian delegation includes around 70 members, comprising technical experts in economic, security and political fields. The talks are expected to begin after the arrival of the US delegation led by Vice President JD Vance, even as tensions linked to Israel-Hezbollah fighting continue to cast a shadow. Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in.

Mojtaba Khamenei has severe and disfiguring wounds, says reportIran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is still recovering from severe injuries sustained in an airstrike that killed his father, while continuing to participate in key decisions through audio meetings.

Iran delegation arrives in Islamabad, says talks start only if US meets preconditionsIranian delegation led by Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf arrives in Islamabad for US talks, saying negotiations will begin only if Washington accepts Tehran's preconditions, signalling a firm stance ahead of discussions.

This hydrogen stove runs on water, not gas and could replace traditional cylindersHydrogen cooking stove India, green hydrogen stove, no LPG cooking. This Made-in-India device runs on hydrogen instead of gas cylinders and is already being explored for kitchens, labs, and large-scale use.

China helping Pak replace AWACS aircraft India shot down in Op Sindoor?Pakistan is likely to acquire Shaanxi KJ-500, an Airborne Warning and Control System (AWACS) aircraft, from China. Negotiations started in June 2025. Just a month before that, India had shot down at least one AWACS aircraft of Pakistan in Operation Sindoor. AWACS is considered a major asset and force multiplier.

The Persian king who captured a Roman emperor the only time in historyA viral meme from the Iran Embassy in India featuring Donald Trump has revived the story of Shapur I, the Persian king who captured Roman emperor Valerian in 260 CE. The imagery flips the script, casting West Asia as the power centre and the West as subdued, echoing an ancient moment when Persia held the upper hand over Rome.

Does 'Books on the Delhi Metro' still matter today?Handcrafted ByOur Editors It's been nearly a decade, and the Delhi metro has quietly become a space for the literary community. IPL Play of the Day: Dhruv Jurel stands guard through Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's assaultDhruv Jurel's calm under pressure outshines the chaos of the chase, guiding RR to victory. While Sooryavanshi dazzled, it was Jurel's composure that sealed the game.

SRH must fix their misfiring top order and underperforming bowling attack on a potentially sluggish Mullanpur pitch to stay competitive.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi leaves Shashi Tharoor spellbound with majestic 78 vs RCBVaibhav Sooryavanshi's extraordinary run in the IPL continues to capture attention well beyond the cricket field, with Congress MP Shashi Tharoor among those left in awe of the teenager's latest knock against RCB.

Look at the ball, not the bowler: Sooryavanshi's mantra vs Bumrah, HazlewoodVaibhav Sooryavanshi revealed that his mantra is to "play the ball" and "not the bowler" after taking apart Josh Hazlewood in a successful 202-run chase against RCB on Friday, days after doing the same to Jasprit Bumrah.

While Rajasthan made it four wins in four, RCB's unbeaten run came to an end on Friday in Guwahati.

SAI canteen shut: Vendor says LPG shortage; Officials say high pricing real issueThe canteen at the Sports Authority of India's headquarters has shut operations, with the vendor attributing it to an LPG shortage, though a well-placed source claimed the real issue could be the "high prices he has been quoting."

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
